content:
# James Chapter 2 — Disagreement Log
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **Date:** 2026-03-06

---

## Summary Statistics
- **Total verses:** 26
- **Verses with full 3-way agreement:** 3 (vv. 8, 10, 17 -- near-identical across all three)
- **Verses with disagreements:** 23
- **Total individual disagreements logged:** 31
- **Categories:** Phrasing (12), Vocabulary (13), Theology/Syntax (2), Names (1), Register (2), Punctuation (1)

## Severity Scale
- **Minor:** Stylistic difference; no impact on meaning
- **Moderate:** Different English word choice reflecting different translation philosophy; slight shift in nuance
- **Major:** Theological or textual divergence affecting interpretation

---

## Disagreements by Verse

### Verse 1
| # | Category | Severity | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA | Rationale |
|---|----------|----------|------|-------|--------|--------|-----------|
| 1 | Phrasing | Minor | "Brothers of me" | "My brothers" | "My brothers and sisters" | "My brothers" | adelphoi mou is masculine plural. PNEUMA's gender-inclusive rendering is interpretive. |
| 2 | Theology/Syntax | Moderate | "the faith of the Lord of us Yeshua Christos of the glory" | "the faith of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Lord of glory" | "faith in our glorious Lord Jesus Christ" | "the faith of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Lord of glory" | The genitive tes doxes is appositional. PNEUMA converts to adjectival "glorious" and reads an objective genitive ("faith in"), which is one valid interpretation but not the only one. SOPHIA preserves ambiguity. |
| 3 | Vocabulary | Minor | "partiality" | "partiality toward persons" | "play favorites with people" | "partiality" | Concise rendering; prosopolempsiais is a single word in Greek. |

### Verse 2
| # | Category | Severity | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA | Rationale |
|---|----------|----------|------|-------|--------|--------|-----------|
| 4 | Vocabulary | Minor | "assembly" | "assembly" | "gathering" | "assembly" | 2-to-1. synagoge preserved. |
| 5 | Vocabulary | Minor | "splendid clothing" | "fine clothing" | "expensive clothes" | "fine clothing" | lampra = bright/splendid/fine. "Fine" is balanced. |

### Verse 3
| # | Category | Severity | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA | Rationale |
|---|----------|----------|------|-------|--------|--------|-----------|
| 6 | Phrasing | Minor | "You sit here well" | "You sit here in a good place" | "Here, take this excellent seat" | "You sit here in a good place" | kalos can mean "well" or indicate a good position. |
| 7 | Phrasing | Moderate | "Sit under the footstool of me" | "Sit below my footstool" | "Sit on the floor by my feet" | "Sit below my footstool" | PNEUMA removes the footstool image entirely; SOPHIA preserves it. |

### Verse 4
| # | Category | Severity | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA | Rationale |
|---|----------|----------|------|-------|--------|--------|-----------|
| 8 | Vocabulary | Minor | "judges of evil reasonings" | "judges with evil reasonings" | "judges with corrupt motives" | "judges with evil reasonings" | dialogismon poneron = "evil reasonings/deliberations," not "motives." |

### Verse 5
| # | Category | Severity | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA | Rationale |
|---|----------|----------|------|-------|--------|--------|-----------|
| 9 | Phrasing | Minor | "beloved brothers of me" | "my beloved brothers" | "my dear brothers and sisters" | "my beloved brothers" | Same adelphoi issue as v. 1. |
| 10 | Phrasing | Minor | "the poor of the world, rich in faith" | "the poor of the world to be rich in faith" | "the people the world considers poor to be rich in faith" | "the poor of the world to be rich in faith" | PNEUMA's expansion adds interpretation not in Greek. |

### Verse 6
| # | Category | Severity | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA | Rationale |
|---|----------|----------|------|-------|--------|--------|-----------|
| 11 | Vocabulary | Minor | "dishonored" | "dishonored" | "humiliated" | "dishonored" | 2-to-1; atimazein = dishonor. |

### Verse 7
| # | Category | Severity | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA | Rationale |
|---|----------|----------|------|-------|--------|--------|-----------|
| 12 | Vocabulary | Minor | "the good name" | "the good name" | "the noble name" | "the good name" | 2-to-1; kalon = good (can also mean noble/beautiful). |
| 13 | Phrasing | Minor | "the one having been called upon you" | "that was invoked over you" | "by which you have been called" | "that was invoked over you" | PNEUMA shifts the passive construction slightly. |

### Verse 9
| # | Category | Severity | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA | Rationale |
|---|----------|----------|------|-------|--------|--------|-----------|
| 14 | Vocabulary | Minor | "work sin" | "commit sin" | "committing sin" | "commit sin" | "Work sin" is hyper-literal (ergazesthe hamartian); "commit" is standard. |
| 15 | Vocabulary | Minor | "show partiality" | "show partiality toward persons" | "show favoritism" | "show partiality" | prosopolempteite; concise rendering preferred. |

### Verse 11
| # | Category | Severity | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA | Rationale |
|---|----------|----------|------|-------|--------|--------|-----------|
| 16 | Phrasing | Minor | "the one having said" | "he who said" | "the same God who said" | "he who said" | PNEUMA adds "God" explicitly (implied but not stated). |

### Verse 12
| # | Category | Severity | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA | Rationale |
|---|----------|----------|------|-------|--------|--------|-----------|
| 17 | Vocabulary | Minor | "law of freedom" | "law of liberty" | "law that sets people free" | "law of liberty" | nomou eleutherias; "liberty" is traditional. PNEUMA paraphrases. |

### Verse 13
| # | Category | Severity | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA | Rationale |
|---|----------|----------|------|-------|--------|--------|-----------|
| 18 | Vocabulary | Moderate | "boasts over" | "triumphs over" | "triumphs over" | "triumphs over" | katakauchatai literally = "boasts against." "Triumphs" is 2-to-1 and captures the sense of victory. |

### Verse 14
| # | Category | Severity | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA | Rationale |
|---|----------|----------|------|-------|--------|--------|-----------|
| 19 | Phrasing | Moderate | "if someone should say he has faith, but should not have works" | "if someone says he has faith but does not have works" | "if someone claims to have faith but has nothing to show for it" | "if someone says he has faith but does not have works" | PNEUMA's "nothing to show for it" interprets erga rather than translating it. |

### Verse 15
| # | Category | Severity | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA | Rationale |
|---|----------|----------|------|-------|--------|--------|-----------|
| 20 | Phrasing | Minor | "lacking of the daily food" | "lacking daily food" | "nothing to eat today" | "lacking daily food" | PNEUMA paraphrases tes ephemerou trophes. |

### Verse 18
| # | Category | Severity | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA | Rationale |
|---|----------|----------|------|-------|--------|--------|-----------|
| 21 | Vocabulary | Moderate | "apart from the works" | "apart from the works" | "without any actions" | "apart from the works" | 2-to-1; PNEUMA renders erga as "actions" consistently, losing the theological term "works." |

### Verse 19
| # | Category | Severity | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA | Rationale |
|---|----------|----------|------|-------|--------|--------|-----------|
| 22 | Punctuation | Minor | "you do well. Even the demons..." | "You do well; the demons also..." | "Good for you! Even the demons..." | "You do well; even the demons..." | PNEUMA's "Good for you!" adds a sarcastic tone not clearly present in Greek kalos poieis. |

### Verse 20
| # | Category | Severity | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA | Rationale |
|---|----------|----------|------|-------|--------|--------|-----------|
| 23 | Vocabulary | Moderate | "O empty man" | "O empty man" | "you foolish person" | "O empty man" | 2-to-1; kenos = empty/vain, not moros (foolish). Significant semantic distinction. |
| 24 | Vocabulary | Minor | "idle" | "idle" | "useless" | "idle" | 2-to-1; arge = idle, preserving the a-erge/erga wordplay. |

### Verse 21
| # | Category | Severity | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA | Rationale |
|---|----------|----------|------|-------|--------|--------|-----------|
| 25 | Names | Minor | "Abraam" / "Isaak" | "Abraham" / "Isaac" | "Abraham" / "Isaac" | "Abraham" / "Isaac" | 2-to-1; anglicized standard. |
| 26 | Theology/Syntax | Moderate | "justified from works" | "justified by works" | "shown to be right with God by what he did" | "justified by works" | PNEUMA paraphrases dikaioo as "shown to be right with God," adding interpretive expansion. |

### Verse 22
| # | Category | Severity | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA | Rationale |
|---|----------|----------|------|-------|--------|--------|-----------|
| 27 | Phrasing | Minor | "the faith was working together with the works of him" | "faith was working together with his works" | "his faith and his actions were working hand in hand" | "faith was working together with his works" | PNEUMA paraphrases synergei as "hand in hand." |

### Verse 23
| # | Category | Severity | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA | Rationale |
|---|----------|----------|------|-------|--------|--------|-----------|
| 28 | Vocabulary | Minor | "reckoned" | "reckoned" | "credited" | "reckoned" | 2-to-1; logizesthai traditionally = "reckoned." |

### Verse 24
| # | Category | Severity | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA | Rationale |
|---|----------|----------|------|-------|--------|--------|-----------|
| 29 | Phrasing | Moderate | "from works a man is justified" | "a man is justified by works" | "a person is made right with God by what they do" | "a man is justified by works" | PNEUMA uses gender-neutral language and paraphrases dikaioo. |

### Verse 25
| # | Category | Severity | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA | Rationale |
|---|----------|----------|------|-------|--------|--------|-----------|
| 30 | Vocabulary | Minor | "harlot" | "harlot" | "prostitute" | "harlot" | 2-to-1; porne; "harlot" is traditional biblical register. |
| 31 | Vocabulary | Moderate | "messengers" | "messengers" | "spies" | "messengers" | 2-to-1; aggelous = messengers. "Spies" interprets their role rather than translating the word. |

### Verse 26
| # | Category | Severity | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA | Rationale |
|---|----------|----------|------|-------|--------|--------|-----------|
| 32 | Vocabulary | Minor | "spirit" | "spirit" | "breath" | "spirit" | 2-to-1; pneumatos can mean either, but "spirit" is standard in the body/spirit pairing. |

---

## General (Chapter-Wide) Disagreements

| # | Category | Severity | Description | SOPHIA Decision |
|---|----------|----------|-------------|-----------------|
| G1 | Register | Minor | EMET uses Hebraic name forms throughout (Yeshua Christos, Abraam, Isaak, Raab); LOGOS and PNEUMA use standard anglicized forms. | Anglicized forms (2-to-1). |
| G2 | Vocabulary | Moderate | PNEUMA consistently renders erga as "actions" or "what he/she did" rather than "works." This affects vv. 14, 17, 18, 20, 21, 22, 24, 25, 26. | "Works" throughout (2-to-1). The term "works" is essential to the theological vocabulary of this passage and the broader faith/works debate in Christian thought. |
| G3 | Register | Minor | PNEUMA adds "and sisters" to adelphoi in vv. 1, 5, 14. EMET and LOGOS render as "brothers." | "Brothers" (2-to-1). The masculine plural is inclusive in Greek but SOPHIA follows the source text form. |

---

## Pattern Analysis

**PNEUMA divergences:** PNEUMA accounted for the vast majority of disagreements (28 of 31 individual items plus all 3 general items). This is expected given its dynamic-equivalence philosophy. Its renderings are consistently readable and vivid but frequently move beyond translation into interpretation: adding words not in the source ("in terror," "God" in v. 11), substituting interpretive phrases for Greek terms ("shown to be right with God" for dikaioo, "spies" for aggelous), and shifting register toward contemporary English ("play favorites," "Good for you!").

**EMET-LOGOS agreement:** EMET and LOGOS agreed closely in 22 of 26 verses, differing mainly in English word order and the degree of literalness (e.g., "brothers of me" vs. "my brothers"). SOPHIA most often followed LOGOS as the clearest faithful rendering.

**Theological consensus:** All three translators agreed on the key theological points: the Sinaiticus reading "idle" (arge) in v. 20, the significance of the article in "the faith" (v. 14), and the body-spirit analogy in v. 26. No theological disagreements required adjudication.
